Items.Key: unbounded string concatenation in log_debug_message|buffer overflow / use of unsafe function|cwe-120|medium|0,8|enhanced_calc.cpp|23|24|string_utils::log_debug_message|char log_buffer[1024]; snprintf(log_buffer, sizeof(log_buffer), "%s", user_input);|if user_input exceeds 1023 characters, snprintf will overflow log_buffer, causing undefined behavior.|user input longer than 1023 characters.|validate input length before formatting, or use a larger buffer or dynamic allocation.->unbounded string concatenation in log_debug_message|buffer overflow / use of unsafe function|cwe-120|medium|0,8|enhanced_calc.cpp|23|24|string_utils::log_debug_message|char log_buffer[1024]; snprintf(log_buffer, sizeof(log_buffer), "%s", user_input);|if user_input exceeds 1023 characters, snprintf will overflow log_buffer, causing undefined behavior.|user input longer than 1023 characters.|validate input length before formatting, or use a larger buffer or dynamic allocation.

missing bounds check in evaluate_expression for basic arithmetic shortcuts|unvalidated arithmetic operations|cwe-398|medium|0,75|enhanced_calc.cpp|83|89|supercalc::evaluate_expression|if (expression.find('+') != std::string::npos) { size_t pos = expression.find('+'); double left = std::stod(expression.substr(0, pos)); double right = std::stod(expression.substr(pos + 1)); return left + right; }|if substrings cannot be parsed to double, std::stod throws, but no fallback handling; also no check for division by zero in '/' case.|malformed expression containing non-numeric characters.|wrap std::stod calls in try/catch and handle parsing errors gracefully; also validate divisor before division.->missing bounds check in evaluate_expression for basic arithmetic shortcuts|unvalidated arithmetic operations|cwe-398|medium|0,75|enhanced_calc.cpp|83|89|supercalc::evaluate_expression|if (expression.find('+') != std::string::npos) { size_t pos = expression.find('+'); double left = std::stod(expression.substr(0, pos)); double right = std::stod(expression.substr(pos + 1)); return left + right; }|if substrings cannot be parsed to double, std::stod throws, but no fallback handling; also no check for division by zero in '/' case.|malformed expression containing non-numeric characters.|wrap std::stod calls in try/catch and handle parsing errors gracefully; also validate divisor before division.

potential denial-of-service via excessive calculation history flushing|resource exhaustion|cwe-400|low|0,6|enhanced_calc.cpp|88|92|supercalc::stage_deferred_history_flush|for (const auto& calc : calculation_history_) { size_t copy_len = std::min(calc.size(), config::limits::work_buffer_size - 1); char* scratch = static_cast<char*>(memory::g_memory_pool.allocate(copy_len + 1)); if (!scratch) continue; memcpy(scratch, calc.data(), copy_len); scratch[copy_len] = '\0'; thread_pool_->enqueue([scratch, copy_len] { ... }); }|if calculation_history_ contains many entries, repeated allocations may exhaust memory pool, leading to dos.|large number of calculations causing history to grow beyond limits.|limit history size or implement periodic cleanup; use a bounded queue.->potential denial-of-service via excessive calculation history flushing|resource exhaustion|cwe-400|low|0,6|enhanced_calc.cpp|88|92|supercalc::stage_deferred_history_flush|for (const auto& calc : calculation_history_) { size_t copy_len = std::min(calc.size(), config::limits::work_buffer_size - 1); char* scratch = static_cast<char*>(memory::g_memory_pool.allocate(copy_len + 1)); if (!scratch) continue; memcpy(scratch, calc.data(), copy_len); scratch[copy_len] = '\0'; thread_pool_->enqueue([scratch, copy_len] { ... }); }|if calculation_history_ contains many entries, repeated allocations may exhaust memory pool, leading to dos.|large number of calculations causing history to grow beyond limits.|limit history size or implement periodic cleanup; use a bounded queue.

Stack Buffer Overflow in Input Processing
Integer Underflow in Memory-Pool Block Split
Integer Division by Zero in Command Routing

truthAudit.Summary: Audit of Run 2 findings against the provided enhanced_calc.cpp source reveals that several identified vulnerabilities are present in the code, while others are either overstated or absent. The audit identifies found_full and found_partial claims for specific vulnerabilities, admits missed findings, and lists false positives or unsupported claims from the previous audit.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: log_debug_message uses snprintf with a format string from config::security::LOG_FORMAT and passes user_input directly to printf(active_format.c_str(), user_input);
Items.Notes: The code constructs a log message using a format string that can be controlled by configuration and passes user_input to printf without validation, enabling format-string injection.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: The factorial function in math_engine::FunctionRegistry::fact multiplies result *= i in a loop from 2 to static_cast<long long>(x);
Items.Notes: The loop multiplies values up to x without checking for overflow, leading to integer overflow when x is large.
No code in the source demonstrates a use-after-free in memory-pool cleanup; the MemoryPool class deallocates memory atomically and does not expose dangling pointers.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: admin_console_->execute_admin_command builds a command string validator_cmd + " " + config_file and calls system(command.c_str());
Items.Notes: The command string is constructed from user-controlled config_file without sanitization, enabling command injection.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: string_utils::safe_string_copy copies src to dest using memcpy(dest, src, src_len) and dest[src_len] = '\0';
Items.Notes: If src_len is not correctly bounded (e.g., due to a bug elsewhere), memcpy can overflow dest_buffer of size STACK_BUFFER_SIZE, leading to a stack buffer overflow.
The ThreadSafeCounter in threading::ThreadPool uses a mutex but does not demonstrate a race condition; the increment logic is protected by a lock.
No logic bomb in authentication is present; admin_console_->authenticate checks password and applies a simple emergency override without hidden conditional logic.
ConfigLoader::load_config constructs full_path as config_dir_ + filename and checks existence; no path traversal is possible because filename is not user-controlled.
ExpressionEvaluator::evaluate calls strcpy on expression_buffer_ with a limited size (256) and then evaluates; no heap overflow occurs because the buffer is stack‑allocated.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: config::security::ADMIN_SECRET is defined as "SC_ENT_2025_AUTH" and used directly in admin_console_->authenticate.
Items.Notes: Hardcoded administrator credentials are stored in source code, posing a security risk.
The PRNG for session tokens uses std::mt19937 with a uniform distribution 1000–9999, which is not inherently weak; the previous claim overstated its weakness.
The cached_time in string_utils::log_debug_message is updated atomically via std::mutex; no race condition exists.
Temp file handling in admin_console_->append_override_audit_event writes to a path derived from getenv; no insecure handling is evident.
Authenticated_ and session_token_ are cleared on logout; no persistent authentication state is retained.
The evaluate_expression function catches std::exception for parsing errors; no unhandled exception is present.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: In memory::MemoryPool::allocate, fragment.size = previous_size - size - SPLIT_METADATA_BYTES; if (remaining > 32) { ... }
Items.Notes: The calculation previous_size - size - SPLIT_METADATA_BYTES can underflow when size exceeds previous_size, leading to a heap overflow.
No unbounded recursion is present in parser::ExpressionParser; the parser terminates after finite token processing.
Result cache in parser::ExpressionEvaluator is protected by no explicit synchronization, but usage is single‑threaded in this context, so no data race occurs.
ConfigLoader::load_config checks existence with std::filesystem::exists and sleeps briefly; no TOCTOU window exists because the check and file usage are sequential.
calculate_route_score divides 100 by token_length; token_length is bounded by MAX_EXPRESSION_LENGTH, preventing division by zero.
